symbols: crown on Mary — her status as Queen of Heaven
nursing infant — the full humanity of Christ, dependent on His mother
vast blue mantle — the heavens draped around the Mother of God
choir of angels and children — the perpetual heavenly worship
ornate Gothic throne — the architecture of heaven, the celestial court
red carpet — royal majesty and the blood of the covenant
description: An illuminated manuscript page showing the Virgin Mary enthroned in an elaborate Gothic architectural setting. She wears a golden crown and an enormous dark blue mantle that cascades down the steps of the throne. The infant Jesus sits on her lap, nursing. At left, a group of angels and children in white and colored robes sing together as a choir. The architecture features intricate Gothic tracery, carved arches, and a shell-shaped niche above. A richly patterned red carpet covers the foreground floor. The colors are vivid — deep blue, red, gold, and white — with extraordinary detail.
theological_description: Fouquet's magnificent illumination presents the Virgin and Child as the center of heavenly worship. The crowned, nursing Madonna embodies the dual theology of Mary's exaltation and Christ's humility — the Queen of Heaven nourishes the King of Kings at her breast. The nursing gesture affirms the full reality of the Incarnation: 'the Word became flesh' (John 1:14) in so radical a way that God depended on a woman's milk. The angelic choir fulfills Isaiah's vision of perpetual heavenly worship: 'Holy, holy, holy is the Lord of hosts' (Isa 6:3). The elaborate Gothic architecture represents the heavenly Jerusalem, 'prepared as a bride adorned for her husband' (Rev 21:2). Mary's enormous blue mantle, filling the composition, suggests her role as mediatrix — she covers the faithful with her protection. The Psalms anticipate this enthroned worship: 'Glorious things are spoken of you, O city of God' (Ps 87:3).

scripture_refs.reason: The Word became flesh — the nursing infant affirming the full Incarnation
Holy, holy, holy — the angelic choir in perpetual worship
The holy city prepared as a bride — the Gothic architecture as heavenly Jerusalem
All generations will call me blessed — Mary crowned and enthroned
